scope: Audit the ten active QoL automations for 30-day efficiency and add reusable measurement plus auto-scaling guardrails.
validation: scripts/measure-automation-efficiency.ps1 ran successfully and reported 10 active QoL automations, 30 starts/day, 900 starts/30 days, 48-minute minimum spacing, 60 minimum accepted passes, 90 stretch accepted passes, and 6.67/10 percent conversion thresholds.
Follow-up closeout validation must parse JSON, run the automation contract checker, scan for conflict markers, and run git diff --check.
efficiency_findings: The schedule is already aggressive enough; more starts are not the immediate efficiency lever.
Accepted-pass and duration telemetry were missing from older manifests, making conversion and overlap measurement weak.
The QoL system is new, so the next weekly calibration must verify each QoL runner has observed evidence or an exact blocker.

automation_efficiency.notes: Retarget QoL focus and manifest telemetry before considering schedule changes.

self_improvement.evidence: Added reusable 30-day efficiency scorecard and documented accepted-pass telemetry fields.

next: Run the 30-day scorecard during Sunday calibration.
Require future QoL manifests to include accepted_mvp_pass, requirement_ids, duration_minutes, output_kind, and automation_efficiency.scale_signal.
Keep the 30-start/day cadence unless conversion, collision, or blocker evidence proves it should change.
